_comment: Example LLM-backend config for the KennisBank judge/extraction (memory-sweep: extract candidates, judge current-vs-quarantine, reconcile, supersede). Read by scripts/_llm.py. This file is OPTIONAL: without it the code default (ollama + gemma4:latest) applies. Copy it to <vault>/.claude/kennisbank-llm.json only to override. Env vars take precedence at runtime (KB_LLM_PROVIDERS, KB_LLM_MODEL, KB_LLM_ENDPOINT, KB_LLM_API_KEY_ENV). See CONFIGURATION.md section 4a.
_pin_your_model: IMPORTANT: the code default is the tag 'gemma4:latest'. If your local Ollama has a differently-tagged model (e.g. 'gemma4:12b'), the sweep probe fails and the heartbeat reports model_unreachable=true even though Ollama is running — capture then silently produces nothing. Pin the tag you actually have (check with `ollama list`) in 'model' below.

_switching._comment: providers is an ORDERED chain; generate() tries each until one returns a non-empty string. Local ('ollama') is default. Cloud providers ('openrouter', 'claude-cli') are OPT-IN: putting them in the chain is explicit consent, and each cloud step logs LOUDLY to stderr, never silently. Per-provider model overrides go in 'models'. Set api_key_env to the NAME of the env var holding the key; never put the key itself here.

claude_cli._note: shells the existing `claude` binary; uses your Claude Code auth, no key needed

local_then_cloud_fallback._note: try local first, fall back to cloud only when local returns empty
